content_en:
Abdullah Ibn Sailam Al-Husayn ibn Sailam was a Jewish rabbi in Yathrib who was widely respected and honoured by the people of the city even by those who were not Jewish. He was known for his piety and goodness, his upright conduct and his truthfulness. Al-Husayn lived a peaceful and gentle life but he was serious, purposeful and organized in the way he spent his time. For a fixed period each day, he would worship, teach and preach in the temple. Then he would spend some time in his orchard, looking after date palms, pruning and pollinating. Thereafter, to increase his understanding and knowledge of his religion, he would devote himself to the study of the Torah. In this study, it is said. he was particularly struck by some verses of the Torah which dealt with the coming of a Prophet who would complete the message of previous Prophets. Al-Husayn therefore took an immediate and keen interest when he heard reports of the appearance of a Prophet in Makkah. He said: "When I heard of the appearance of the Messenger of God, peace be on him, I began to make enquiries about his name, his genealogy, his characteristics, his time and place and I began to compare this information with what is contained m our books. From these enquiries, I became convinced about the authenticity of his prophethood and I affirmed the truth of his mission. However, I concealed my conclusions from the Jews. I held my tongue... Then came the day when the Prophet, peace be on him, left Makkah and headed for Yathrib. When he reached Yathrib and stopped at Quba, a man came rushing into the city, calling out to people and announcing the arrival of the Prophet. At that moment, I was at the top of a palm tree doing some work. My aunt, Khalidah bint al-Harith, was sitting under the tree. On hearing the news, I shouted: 'Allahu Akbar! Allahu Akbar! (God is Great! God is Great!' When my aunt heard my takbir, she remonstrated with me: 'May God frustrate you...By God, if you had heard that Moses was coming you would not have been more enthusiastic.' 'Auntie, he is really, by God, the 'brother' of Moses and follows his religion. He was sent with the same mission as Moses.' She was silent for a while and then said: 'Is he the Prophet about whom you spoke to us who would be sent to confirm the truth preached by previous (Prophets) and complete the message of his Lord?' 'Yes,' I replied. Without any delay or hesitation, I went out to meet the Prophet. I saw crowds of people at his door. I moved about in the crowds until I reached close to him. The first words I heard him say were: 'O people! Spread peace...Share food...Pray during the night while people (normally) sleep... and you will enter Paradise in peace...' I looked at him closely. I scrutinized him and was convinced that his face was not that of an imposter. I went closer to him and made the declaration of faith that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad

is the Messenger of Allah. The Prophet turned to me and asked: 'What is your name?' 'Al-Husayn ibn Sailam,' I replied. 'Instead, it is (now) Abdullah ibn Sallam,' he said (giving me a new name). 'Yes,' I agreed. 'Abdullah ibn Sailam (it shall be). By Him who has sent you with the Truth, I do not wish to have another name after this day.' I returned home and introduced Islam to my wife, my children and the rest of my household. They all accepted Islam including my aunt KhaIidah who was then an old lady. However, I advised them then to conceal our acceptance of Islam from the Jews until I gave them permission. They agreed. Subsequently, I went back to the Prophet, peace be on him, and said: 'O Messenger of God! The Jews are a people (inclined to) slander and falsehood. I want you to invite their most prominent men to meet you. (During the meeting however), you should keep me concealed from them in one of your rooms. Ask them then about my status among them before they find out of my acceptance of Islam. Then invite them to Islam. If they were to know that I have become a Muslim, they would denounce me and accuse me of everything base and slander me.' The Prophet kept me in one of his rooms and invited the prominent Jewish personalities to visit him. He introduced Islam to them and urged them to have faith in God...They began to dispute and argue with him about the Truth. When he realized that they were not inclined to accept Islam, he put the question to them: 'What is the status of Al-Husayn ibn Sailam among you?' 'He is our sayyid (leader) and the son of our sayyid. He is our rabbi and our alim (scholar), the son of our rabbi and alim.' 'If you come to know that he has accepted Islam, would you accept Islam also?' asked the Prophet. 'God forbid! He would not accept Islam. May God protect him from accepting Islam,' they said (horrified). At this point I came out in full view of them and announced: 'O assembly of Jews! Be conscious of God and accept what Muhammad has brought. By God, you certainly know that he is the Messenger of God and you can find prophecies about him and mention of his name and characteristics in your Torah. I for my part declare that he is the Messenger of God. I have faith in him and believe that he is true. I know him.' 'You are a liar,' they shouted. 'By God, you are evil and ignorant, the son of an evil and ignorant person.' And they continued to heap every conceivable abuse on me..." Abdullah ibn Sailam approached Islam with a soul thirsty for knowledge. He was passionately devoted to the Quran and spent much time reciting and studying its beautiful and sublime verses. He was deeply attached to the noble Prophet and was constantly in his company. Much of his time he spent in the masjid, engaged in worship, in learning and in teaching. He was known

for his sweet, moving and effective way of teaching study circles of Sahabah who assembled regularly in the Prophet's mosque. Abdullah ibn Sallam was known among the Sahabah as a man from ahl-al-Jannah "- the people of Paradise. This was because of his determination on the advice of the Prophet to hold steadfastly to the "most trustworthy handhold" that is belief in and total submission to God.
content_id:
Al-Husayn ibn Salam adalah seorang rabi Yahudi di Yathrib yang sangat dihormati dan dimuliakan oleh penduduk kota itu, bahkan oleh mereka yang bukan Yahudi. Beliau dikenal karena ketakwaan dan kebaikannya, perilakunya yang lurus, dan kejujurannya. Al-Husayn menjalani kehidupan yang damai dan lembut, namun serius, bertujuan, dan teratur dalam caranya menghabiskan waktu. Selama waktu tertentu setiap harinya, beliau beribadah, mengajar, dan berkhutbah di kuil. Kemudian beliau menghabiskan beberapa waktu di kebunnya, merawat pohon kurma, memangkas dan membuahi. Setelah itu, untuk meningkatkan pemahaman dan pengetahuannya tentang agamanya, beliau mendedikasikan diri untuk mempelajari Taurat. Dalam kajian itu, konon beliau sangat terkesan oleh beberapa ayat Taurat yang membahas tentang datangnya seorang Nabi yang akan menyempurnakan risalah para nabi sebelumnya. Al-Husayn dengan demikian sangat tertarik dan segera menaruh perhatian ketika mendengar laporan tentang kemunculan seorang Nabi di Makkah.

Beliau berkata: "Ketika aku mendengar tentang kemunculan Rasulullah ☯, aku mulai mencari informasi tentang namanya, silsilahnya, sifat-sifatnya, zamannya, dan tempatnya, lalu mulai membandingkan informasi ini dengan apa yang terkandung dalam kitab-kitab kami. Dari penyelidikan-penyelidikan ini, aku menjadi yakin tentang keautentikan kenabiannya dan aku menegaskan kebenaran misinya. Namun aku menyembunyikan kesimpulanku dari orang-orang Yahudi. Aku menahan lidahku... Kemudian tibalah hari ketika Nabi ☯ meninggalkan Makkah menuju Yathrib. Ketika beliau tiba di Yathrib dan singgah di Quba, seorang pria berlari masuk ke kota, berseru kepada orang-orang dan mengumumkan kedatangan Nabi ☯. Saat itu aku sedang berada di atas pohon kurma mengerjakan sesuatu. Bibiku, Khalidah bint al-Harith, sedang duduk di bawah pohon. Ketika mendengar berita itu, aku berseru: 'Allahu Akbar! Allahu Akbar!' Ketika bibiku mendengar takbirku, beliau menegurku: 'Semoga Allah mengecewakanmu... Demi Allah, seandainya kamu mendengar bahwa Musa datang pun kamu tidak akan lebih antusias.' 'Bibi, dia benar-benar, demi Allah, saudara Musa dan mengikuti agamanya. Beliau diutus dengan misi yang sama dengan Musa.' Beliau diam sebentar kemudian berkata: 'Apakah dia adalah Nabi yang kamu ceritakan kepada kami yang akan diutus untuk mengonfirmasi kebenaran yang diajarkan oleh para Nabi sebelumnya dan menyempurnakan risalah Tuhannya?' 'Ya,' jawabku."

Tanpa penundaan, al-Husayn pergi menemui Nabi ☯. Beliau melihat kerumunan orang di pintunya dan bergerak di antara kerumunan hingga dekat dengannya. Kata-kata pertama yang beliau dengar dari Nabi ☯ adalah: "Wahai manusia! Sebarkanlah salam... Bagikanlah makanan... Shalatlah di malam hari ketika orang-orang tidur... dan kamu akan masuk Surga dalam keadaan damai." Beliau memperhatikan Nabi ☯ dengan seksama dan meyakini bahwa wajahnya bukanlah wajah seorang penipu. Beliau pun mendekatinya dan mengucapkan syahadat bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah dan bahwa Muhammad adalah Rasulullah ☯. Nabi ☯ berpaling kepadanya dan bertanya: "Siapa namamu?" "Al-Husayn ibn Salam," jawabnya. "Sebaliknya, namamu sekarang adalah Abdullah ibn Salam," sabda beliau. "Ya," beliau setuju. "Abdullah ibn Salam, demi Dia yang telah mengutusmu dengan Kebenaran, aku tidak ingin memiliki nama lain setelah hari ini." Beliau kembali ke rumah dan memperkenalkan Islam kepada istri, anak-anak, dan seluruh anggota rumah tangganya. Mereka semua menerima Islam termasuk bibiku Khalidah yang saat itu sudah tua. Namun beliau menyarankan mereka untuk menyembunyikan penerimaan Islam mereka dari orang-orang Yahudi sampai beliau memberi izin. Mereka setuju.

Selanjutnya, beliau kembali kepada Nabi ☯ dan berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah ☯! Orang-orang Yahudi adalah kaum yang suka memfitnah dan berdusta. Aku ingin engkau mengundang tokoh-tokoh terkemuka mereka untuk menemuimu. Namun sembunyikan aku dari mereka di salah satu kamarmu. Tanyakan kepada mereka tentang kedudukanku di antara mereka sebelum mereka mengetahui penerimaanku terhadap Islam. Kemudian ajaklah mereka masuk Islam. Jika mereka mengetahui bahwa aku telah menjadi Muslim, mereka akan mencercaku dan menuduhku dengan segala macam keburukan serta memfitnahku." Nabi ☯ menyembunyikannya di salah satu kamarnya dan mengundang tokoh-tokoh Yahudi terkemuka untuk berkunjung. Nabi ☯ memperkenalkan Islam kepada mereka dan mendorong mereka untuk beriman kepada Allah. Mereka mulai berdebat tentang Kebenaran. Ketika Nabi ☯ menyadari bahwa mereka tidak berniat menerima Islam, beliau bertanya kepada mereka: "Apa kedudukan al-Husayn ibn Salam di antara kalian?" "Dia adalah sayyid kami dan putra sayyid kami. Dia adalah rabbi kami dan alim kami, putra rabbi dan alim kami." "Jika kalian mengetahui bahwa ia telah menerima Islam, apakah kalian juga akan menerima Islam?" tanya Nabi ☯. "Na'udzubillah! Dia tidak akan menerima Islam. Semoga Allah melindunginya dari menerima Islam," kata mereka dengan ngeri. Saat itulah Abdullah ibn Salam keluar dan mengumumkan: "Wahai orang-orang Yahudi! Bertakwalah kepada Allah dan terimalah apa yang dibawa Muhammad ☯. Demi Allah, kalian pasti mengetahui bahwa dia adalah Rasulullah ☯ dan kalian dapat menemukan nubuat-nubuat tentang dirinya dan penyebutan namanya serta sifat-sifatnya dalam Taurat kalian. Aku dari pihakku menyatakan bahwa dia adalah Rasulullah ☯. Aku beriman kepadanya dan memercayai bahwa dia benar. Aku mengenalinya." "Kamu pembohong," teriak mereka. "Demi Allah, kamu jahat dan bodoh, anak dari orang yang jahat dan bodoh." Dan mereka terus menumpahkan setiap cacian yang bisa mereka bayangkan kepadanya.

Abdullah ibn Salam (ra) mendekati Islam dengan jiwa yang haus akan ilmu. Beliau sangat tekun kepada Al-Quran dan menghabiskan banyak waktu untuk membaca dan mengkaji ayat-ayatnya yang indah dan agung. Beliau sangat lekat dengan Nabi ☯ yang mulia dan selalu berada di sisi beliau. Banyak waktunya beliau habiskan di masjid, beribadah, belajar, dan mengajar. Beliau dikenal karena cara mengajarnya yang memikat, mengharukan, dan efektif dalam halaqah-halaqah ilmu yang secara rutin dihadiri para Sahabat di masjid Nabi ☯. Abdullah ibn Salam dikenal di antara para Sahabat sebagai seorang dari ahl al-Jannah, penduduk Surga. Hal ini karena tekadnya atas saran Nabi ☯ untuk berpegang teguh pada "pegangan yang paling kuat", yaitu keimanan dan ketundukan total kepada Allah.
